notes: Generated by specbridge-intake on 2026-06-10. Task: SpecBridge Operator Queue Hygiene + Next Task Selector. Goal: Add an explicit operator-governed queue hygiene layer so SpecBridge can distinguish real next tasks from rejected, deferred, superseded, or blocked GitHub issues. Immediate motivation: issue #194 remains open on GitHub but was rejected by the operator and must not be selected as the next task. Implement: 1) .specbridge/policies/operator-task-decisions.json recording operator decisions (not_planned, deferred, superseded, blocked) with issue, task_id, reason, decided_by, decided_at; issue #194 recorded as not_planned. 2) specbridge-next-task CLI command: reads current goal state and operator decisions, reports eligible tasks, excluded tasks and recommended action; must not call GitHub and must not mutate repository state. 3) scripts/validate-operator-task-decisions.ps1 validating schema, required fields, enum values, timestamps, duplicates and reason presence; added to smoke. 4) Studio dashboard Operator Queue section: eligible count, excluded list, recommended action. 5) README: open GitHub issues are not automatically eligible SpecBridge tasks; operator decisions are authoritative. Boundaries: do not implement the digital twin, do not rebase or revive its branch, do not call external APIs, do not close GitHub issues from the CLI, do not change merge policy. Acceptance: #194 excluded by next-task, create_new_operator_task recommended when no eligible work, validator fails on malformed records, smoke green.
